title: Automation stopped firing after teammate updated shared config
issue_description: A user reports that their approval automation stopped running two days ago. Nothing changed on their end. A teammate updated the trigger conditions on a shared automation the same afternoon.

events.detail: Trigger filter modified: changed condition from 'status = submitted' to 'status = submitted AND priority = high'
Automation saved and re-enabled after config update
Record status set to 'submitted' — expected automation to fire
Trigger condition not met — 'priority' field is null, condition requires 'high'

expected_output.root_cause: A shared automation trigger condition was tightened without notifying all users who depend on it. The new condition requires 'priority = high', but affected records have a null priority field, so the trigger silently skips.
expected_output.top_fix: Set the priority field on affected records to 'high', or revert the trigger condition to its original form.

title: User lost access to a workspace after a role change
issue_description: A user says they can no longer access the Finance workspace. They haven't changed anything. An admin updated the 'Finance Viewer' role permissions earlier that day.

events.detail: Role 'Finance Viewer' updated — removed access to workspace 'Finance' from inherited permissions
Active sessions for all users with role 'Finance Viewer' invalidated to apply permission change
User attempted to access workspace 'Finance' — received 403 error

expected_output.root_cause: A role-level permission change removed workspace access for all users assigned to 'Finance Viewer'. The user's individual settings were not changed — the effective permission dropped because the role definition changed.
expected_output.top_fix: Restore access to the 'Finance' workspace on the 'Finance Viewer' role, or move the user to a role that retains the correct access.

title: Record values appear to have rolled back after a bulk import
issue_description: Several users report that field values they updated last week have reverted to old values. The changes seem to have disappeared. A bulk import ran overnight.

events.detail: Field 'contract_value' updated from 12000 to 18500 on record #4421
Overnight bulk import initiated — source: CRM export snapshot from 2024-11-09
Field 'contract_value' on record #4421 set to 12000 — import source treated as authoritative
User opened record #4421 and noticed 'contract_value' reverted to 12000

expected_output.root_cause: The overnight import used a snapshot from the previous day, before the manual updates were made. Because the import treats its source as authoritative, it overwrote the more recent manual changes without a conflict warning.
expected_output.top_fix: Re-apply the manual updates to affected records and configure the import to use 'skip if newer' logic to prevent overwrites.
